MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NRT REO EXPERTS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Jonathan Joiner of the Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt (391.6496) for plaintiff and responding party; Arthur J. Harris of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (788.1900) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication and summary judgment denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden. The litigation privilege does not apply. See Delois v. Barrett Block Partners (2009) 177 Cal. App. 4th. 940. Further, there is a triable issue of fact as to whether the prelitigation communications related to litigation that was contemplated in good faith. See Action Apartment Assoc. v. City of Santa Monica (2007) 41 Cal. 4th. 1232. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT COLDWELL BANKER, VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC., ROBERT KINOSHITA II, and, REALOGY CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jonathan Joiner, Esq. of Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt, (415) 391-649, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to respond. A pre-litigation communication is privileged only when it relates to litigation that is contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration. (See Birkner vs. Lam (2007) 156 Cal.App. 4th 275.) A factual issue remains regarding the application of the litigation privilege. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR #5208

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Continued To Sep-14-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT COLDWELL BANKER, VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC., ROBERT KINOSHITA, and, REALOGY CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to September 14, 2009 on the Court's own motion. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
